structural_observations: The JSON object has twelve top-level keys and identifies schema_version 2.0.0, record_kind unit_art_board_review, record_status TEMPLATE, a canonical criteria path, and criteria version 2026-07-21-v1.

The unit object contains fifteen fields covering identity, combat role, frontliner status, sex-design category, visual lane, horror status, primary and secondary traits, gameplay hook, dominant prop or anatomy, protection logic, supernatural cost, and villain emotion family.

The psychology object contains ten fields: core wound, private want, primary fear, survival strategy, villainous distortion, current emotional state, emotional contradiction, face direction, pose direction, and forbidden read.
The evidence object contains five named image artifacts plus three_nearest_neighbors and roster_comparison_boards. Each named artifact carries path, SHA-256, and media type placeholders.

The silhouette artifact declares option labels A, B, and C and a selected-option slot. The face and 96-pixel artifacts carry source-master hash slots and fixed derivation-method labels; the 96-pixel artifact additionally declares width and height as 96.

The reviews array contains exactly three parallel seat objects ordered A, B, and C. Each has an independent-round record receipt, fifteen verdict slots, six visible-face evidence fields, six professional-standard fields, blockers, readiness, and a seven-field cross-examination object.
The approval object contains three derived aggregate flags, a blocker ledger, board status, user-approved state, and user-approval provenance.
semantic_observations: The template separates identity and intended psychology from evidence receipts, seat-authored review material, cross-examination, and final approval state.

All unit-specific, psychology, reviewer, verdict, professional-band, blocker, readiness, and approval values are blank placeholders; criteria_sha256 and board_lineage_id are also null in the template state.
Independent review and cross-examination are represented as external JSON artifacts with path and hash receipts rather than embedded review transcripts.
The professional-standard structure reserves both an overall band and a critical-dimensions array, together with benchmark comparison, rejection case, excellence gap, and confidence fields.
Board status and user approval are represented separately, with a dedicated provenance slot for the latter.
uncertainties: The template is an example JSON record rather than a JSON Schema, so required fields, allowed enum values, conditional applicability, and aggregation rules are not expressed within E054 itself.
The template alone does not state which of the fifteen verdicts are always required versus conditionally applicable.
three_nearest_neighbors is an array of identifiers without embedded path or hash receipts, and the required structure of roster_comparison_boards is not specified inside the blank array.
The vellum_first_comparison artifact has a generic image receipt but no separate source-reference identity or derivation-provenance fields in this template.
No completed instance is part of E054, so populated-value behavior and cross-field consistency cannot be observed from this artifact alone.

structural_observations: The Python file is 973 lines and exposes separate validation paths for a blank template, a completed record, referenced files, semantic self-tests, and file-evidence self-tests.

The validator freezes four visual lanes, four sex-design categories, eight protection-logic values, five villain-emotion families, ten required verdicts, five conditional verdicts, ten psychology fields, six visible-face evidence fields, five professional bands, and eight required professional dimensions.
Command-line parsing requires exactly one of --template or --record, with optional --self-test and --check-files switches.

Template validation checks version, kind, template status, authority, criteria version, top-level object types, exactly three ordered seats, presence of all verdict keys, review-artifact media declarations, cross-examination shape, psychology keys, evidence keys and image media declarations, and the user-approval-provenance key.

Completed-record validation adds unit text and enum checks, visual-lane and horror cross-field rules, ten substantive psychology strings, artifact receipts, derivative source hashes, fixed derivation labels, exact declared 96-by-96 dimensions, three unique neighbor identifiers, at least one roster board, and distinct paths and hashes for visual evidence.

Each completed seat requires a unique reviewer identifier, a distinct independent-round JSON receipt, PASS or FAIL values for required and applicable conditional verdicts, NOT_APPLICABLE for inapplicable conditional verdicts, substantive visible-face evidence, professional-band material, blockers, readiness, and a cross-examination receipt and outcome.
The overall professional band is checked against the lowest critical-dimension band. Professional readiness requires that lowest band to be at least PROFESSIONAL and confidence to be HIGH or MEDIUM.

Per-seat readiness is recomputed from gates, professional status, blockers, and cleared cross-examination. Approval aggregates and board status are then recomputed from all three seats and blocker state.
USER_APPROVED records require READY board status, user_approved true, and structured provenance containing a UUID thread id, substantive turn and timestamp strings, and an approval-text SHA-256.

Optional file checking verifies existence and declared hashes, PNG extension, signature, IHDR, positive dimensions, exact 96-by-96 dimensions for the native check, and nonempty parseable JSON review artifacts.

The self-test constructs a syntactically valid BOARD_REVIEWED control, accepts an honest conditional failure when its derived status is NOT_READY, and applies negative mutations for gate bypasses, missing psychology, derivative-hash mismatch, reviewer duplication, false aggregates, invalid lanes, unresolved cross-examination, criteria-hash mismatch, shared review evidence, placeholder professional evidence, low-confidence readiness, and unstructured user approval.
The file-evidence self-test creates temporary PNG and JSON artifacts, validates a file-backed control, and checks that a non-image criteria file cannot pass as PNG visual evidence.
semantic_observations: Conditional applicability is derived from female design, deliberate-horror status, frontliner status, and presence of the Blessed trait in either primary or secondary channels.

A non-horrific female record is required to use the hot_adult_woman lane, while deliberate-horror records are required to use one of the two horror lanes; a female frontliner cannot use not_applicable protection logic.
An honest FAIL is structurally valid when downstream readiness and aggregate fields reflect that failure; the validator rejects inconsistency rather than requiring every submitted record to be READY.
Without --check-files, artifact path and hash fields are checked for text, syntax, declared media type, uniqueness, and declared provenance, but referenced bytes are not opened.

Even with --check-files, the image checks establish PNG container identity and dimensions rather than visual content, crop boundaries, silhouette-option content, or whether a declared crop or containment transform was actually applied.
The source-master provenance checks compare declared SHA-256 strings for face and 96-pixel derivatives; they do not reconstruct or compare the image transformation.
The blocker ledger participates in board-status derivation, but no rule in E055 requires its entries to equal the union of seat blockers and surviving cross-examination blockers.

validate_template checks a subset of the blank record's nested contract: it does not enumerate the fifteen unit keys, visible-face fields, professional-standard fields, or approval aggregate fields beyond user_approval_provenance.

ROOT is assigned Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]. At E055's frozen locator this resolves to docs/art, after which DEFAULT_TEMPLATE and CRITERIA_PATH append docs/art again, yielding duplicated docs/art/docs/art path segments.
DEFAULT_TEMPLATE names docs/art/unit_art_board_review_template.json, while the directly paired E054 artifact is docs/art/phase2_calibration/audit_contract/unit_template.json.
uncertainties: Only E055 and E054 were inspected, so it is unknown whether an external launcher copies E055 to a different directory before execution or otherwise compensates for the in-place ROOT calculation.
The separately named DEFAULT_TEMPLATE target was not opened, so its intended relationship to E054 cannot be established from the assigned artifacts.
The validator was inspected statically and its external criteria or default-template dependencies were not opened under this checkpoint's restricted scope.
Semantic truth of authored prose, visual quality, nearest-neighbor identity, and benchmark appropriateness are outside the checks implemented by this file.
